comment: **Raised 2026-07-01** from medium: both terms are now directly verified against their own primary sources (`xwkont:ref:ceusters-smith-2015`, `xwkont:ref:gangemi-dul`), both use generic-dependence/non-physical treatment for informational content independent of a specific carrier. Not raised to high because the two apparatus still differ in detail (IAO's single `Information Content Entity` vs. DUL's `InformationObject`/`InformationEntity`/`InformationRealization` three-way split).

Structural mismatch: BFO(IAO) separates content from carrier via generic dependence; SUMO ties content directly to a physical carrier. Whether they denote overlapping extension despite the different modeling choice is unresolved.

No UFO correspondence identified with confidence; a full-text check of the extracted 2021 paper (2026-07-01) found zero mentions of "document" or "information object," strengthening but not confirming the absence. Recorded to make the gap explicit rather than silently omitted.

Both treat informational content as generically dependent on, but distinct from, a physical carrier (YAMATO: "not concrete yet as it is... becomes a physical individual only when it becomes a representing thing"; BFO(IAO): "generically dependent on some material entity"). YAMATO's own text explicitly names BFO's treatment as a point of comparison, strengthening this beyond an editorial observation alone. Not `high` because YAMATO's own OWL is not fetched (quotation-only citation) and the two apparatuses differ in elaboration (YAMATO's form/content split vs. IAO's single-class aboutness relation).

Structurally the closest pairing found in this crosswalk: both sources independently split an abstract content-bearing class from its concrete realization (DUL: `InformationObject`/`InformationRealization`; YAMATO: `Representation`/`Representing thing`), per `note-004`. `medium-high`, not `high`, for the same sourcing-completeness reason as `mapping-004` (see `uncertainty-005`): YAMATO's own OWL/Hozo files are not fetched, so this is cited by quotation from the 2010 report, not verified against a formal class. Separately disclosed, but not the basis for the confidence value: this pairing is this crosswalk's own observed convergence, not a comparison either source's authors draw themselves (contrast `mapping-004`, where YAMATO explicitly cites BFO) — a confidently-known fact, not an open question, so it does not itself justify a hedged confidence value.

No GFO correspondence identified with confidence; `Symbol`/`Symbol_structure` were considered and rejected — they sit on GFO's universal/type side (`Category`), not the individual/content-bearing side BFO(IAO)/DOLCE(DUL)/YAMATO's analogs occupy. Recorded explicitly rather than silently omitted, per this crosswalk's established practice for UFO.

No TUpper correspondence identified; `tupper.all.owl` and `TUpper-Terms.html`, searched directly, contain zero occurrences of "information," "content," or "represent." The most clear-cut absence of any source in this crosswalk.

No GUM correspondence identified with confidence; `Signification`/`Symbolization` were considered and rejected — both are relation types (subclasses of `Intensive`), not object/entity types, a categorically different kind of thing than an Information-Object-equivalent class.
